behaviors.description: A cell containing =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of values in cells A1 through A10
behaviors.justification: Testing SUM in isolation with mocked cell values would miss failures in how the engine resolves range references (A1:A10) and integrates cell lookup with formula evaluation. A real failure requires wiring together the range parser, cell resolver, and evaluator with actual cell objects. Mocking cell lookup hides bugs in reference resolution, type coercion, and cascading updates.
behaviors.test_description: Create a sheet with cells A1–A10 populated with numeric values (1, 2, ..., 10), add a cell with formula =SUM(A1:A10), evaluate the sheet, and assert result is 55. Test with floating-point values, mixed numeric types, and edge cases (zeros, negatives) to catch precision and coercion bugs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multiple modules (sheet, cell objects, parser, evaluator) wired together, not just function logic.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula produces the correct cascaded result
behaviors.justification: Mocking cell B1's result would hide bugs in how the engine tracks and resolves formula-to-formula dependencies and evaluation order. The real failure is 'when B1 depends on A1, and C1 depends on B1, does C1 get the correct value?' This requires multiple cell objects wired together and proper dependency resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Create A1=5, B1=A1*2, C1=B1+1. Evaluate C1 and verify result is 11. Change A1 to 10 and verify C1 updates to 21. Test with longer chains (4+ levels) and multiple independent branches to catch evaluation-order bugs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires a full sheet with multiple dependent formulas and dependency tracking.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency and returns a circular reference error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: Unit-testing cycle detection on a graph structure might catch some bugs, but the real failure is when the engine encounters a circular reference during formula evaluation and either loops infinitely or returns a wrong value. Mocking cell resolution hides issues in how the evaluator triggers cycle detection at runtime. Integration testing with actual circular cells is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create A1=B1+1, B1=A1+1, and attempt to evaluate either. Verify the engine returns a circular reference error without hanging. Test longer cycles (A→B→C→A) and self-loops (A→A) to catch incomplete cycle detection.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual cells with circular references and observation of error behavior.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with exact match returns the correct value, and returns an error when the key is not found
behaviors.justification: Unit testing VLOOKUP with hardcoded table arrays might pass but miss bugs in how the engine resolves range references (A1:B10) into table structures, parses column indices, or handles range boundaries. The real failure boundary includes range resolution integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a two-column lookup table in A1:B10 with (ID, Name) pairs. Create formula =VLOOKUP(5, A1:B10, 2, 0) and verify correct name is returned. Test exact match, missing key (expect error), column index out of bounds, and different match flags.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual cells forming the lookup table and range resolution.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions
behaviors.justification: Testing IF logic with hardcoded condition values can catch some bugs, but the example formula =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low") requires resolving cell reference A1 and passing its value to the condition evaluator. Mocking A1 hides bugs in type coercion (string vs. number comparison) or issues in how cell values are resolved.
behaviors.test_description: Create cell A1 and test with values 5, 15, 25 separately. For each, create formula =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low") and verify results are "low", "medium", "high" respectively. Test with boolean, text, and edge-case values to catch type coercion bugs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires cell references and proper type casting in condition evaluation.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings
behaviors.justification: While CONCATENATE logic with hardcoded empty strings can be unit-tested, the issue is specifically about how empty CELLS are handled in the spreadsheet context. A real failure is when the engine represents an empty cell (no value) incorrectly to CONCATENATE, leading to wrong output. Integration testing with actual empty cells ensures the engine correctly represents empty-cell semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1="hello", B1=(empty), C1="world", and formula =CONCATENATE(A1, B1, C1). Verify result is "helloworld" (empty cell skipped, not padded). Test with various empty/non-empty combinations and different separators to catch edge cases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual cells with varying states (populated, empty) and proper representation.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: Testing cross-sheet reference parsing (e.g., 'Sheet2!A1') is unit-testable, but the real failure is whether the engine actually resolves to the correct sheet object and cell. Mocking sheet lookup hides bugs in sheet registration, name matching, or cell resolution across sheets.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with two sheets (Sheet1, Sheet2). In Sheet2.A1, put value 10. In Sheet1, create formula =Sheet2!A1+5. Evaluate and verify result is 15. Test with sheet names containing spaces, numeric suffixes, and multi-level references.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multiple sheet objects and cross-sheet reference resolution.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order
behaviors.justification: Testing topological sort on a dependency graph is unit-testable, but the real failure is when the engine updates a cell and dependents don't recalculate in the correct order, leading to stale or out-of-order results. Mocking cell updates hides issues in how changes propagate through the graph.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1=5, B1=A1*2 (=10), C1=B1+A1 (=15), D1=C1*B1 (=150). Change A1 to 10 and verify B1, C1, D1 update to 20, 30, 600 respectively in a single pass. Test with deeper graphs and multiple independent branches.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires full sheet with dependency tracking and observing recalculation order.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: Testing the undo mechanism on a command history structure is unit-testable, but the real failure is when undo doesn't restore both the original formula text AND all previously computed values. Mocking cell state hides bugs in snapshot restoration or selective state rollback.
behaviors.test_description: Create A1=5, B1=A1*2 (=10), C1=B1+1 (=11). Edit B1 to A1*3, triggering recalculation (B1→15, C1→16). Call undo and verify B1's formula reverts to "=A1*2" and C1 recomputes to 11. Test multi-step undo and alternating edit/undo sequences.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires editor, evaluator, and undo system integration with observation of both formula and computed state.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas with no data loss or floating-point precision change
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires file I/O and persistence. Mocking the file system hides serialization bugs (incorrect binary encoding, floating-point precision loss, formula corruption) or file write/read errors. Real failures include data loss on disk, encoding mismatches, or precision loss in floating-point round-tripping. System-level file I/O is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with mixed types: integers, floating-point (e.g., 0.1+0.2), text, formulas (=A1+B1), empty cells, and cross-sheet references. Save to temporary file, delete in-memory workbook, load from file, and verify all cells have identical values and formulas including floating-point precision. Test large workbooks.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual file I/O and binary serialization/deserialization.
